sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, presents with a 2-year history of chronic back pain, initially started after moving furniture without any precipitating trauma.
He describes the pain as a sharp stabbing pain in his back, radiating down to his right leg, specifically to his right toe, and is exacerbated by sitting.
He also reports numbness and tingling in his right leg, especially to his foot.
He denies loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area, weakness, or loss of control of bladder or bowels.
Past medical history: The patient has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past, but these have been temporary solutions.
He has also been considering surgery, but would like to avoid it.
Review of systems: He reports that his daily activities are limited due to back pain, which has led to weight gain.
He used to enjoy playing golf but has been unable to do so due to his condition.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: On musculoskeletal back exam, there is midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
On neurological examination, there is decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain; Herniated disk L4-L5; Radiculopathy; Chronic pain; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid, which will be administered under fluoroscopy to help localize the exact area of pain.
This should help alleviate the inflammation causing the pain.
I will monitor the patient's response to the injection and schedule follow-up appointments as needed.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the injection, and we will reassess his symptoms and adjust the treatment plan accordingly.
